frameworks.name: decoded fMRI neurofeedback
frameworks.one_line: closed-loop real-time decoding used to causally modulate pain-related multivoxel patterns

frameworks.name: multivoxel brain decoders (SIIPS, NPS)
frameworks.one_line: stimulus-intensity-independent and neurologic pain signatures as decoding targets

core_question: Can real-time neurofeedback modulating pain-related fMRI multivoxel patterns causally change subjective pain perception?
key_assumptions: SIIPS and NPS index dissociable components of pain experience
MNI-space decoders can be applied online without subject-specific training
closed-loop feedback enables learned voluntary control of neural patterns
manipulations: double-blinded up- vs down-regulation of SIIPS via neurofeedback
regulation target signature (SIIPS vs NPS) tracked independently
key_findings: subjective pain ratings predictable in real time from SIIPS+NPS
participants learned to downregulate SIIPS independently of NPS
training success correlated with reduced perceived pain intensity after intervention
interpretations: a priori fMRI decoders support efficient closed-loop modulation of pain
results provide causal evidence linking SIIPS expression to subjective pain
